fault_hypothesis: Line 71 uses a strict greater-than comparison (`> limit`) instead of `>= limit`, so when `len(active) == limit` the gate incorrectly allows the request instead of denying it.

spec.expr: not (len(active__1) == limit and len(active__1) > limit == False)

constraints.intent: When active count equals limit, the condition len(active__1) > limit must be True (i.e., the comparison should be >= not >); this is False on failing tests.
After the else branch (gate allows), active count must be strictly less than limit; if equal it should have been denied.
Whenever active count meets or exceeds limit, the guard condition must trigger denial; the off-by-one means == limit slips through.

spec.expr: not (__cbfl_result[0] is True and len(active__1) >= limit)

constraints.intent: The function must never return allowed=True when the active count is at or above the limit.
